title: Global Food Prices Face New Pressure from 'Super' El Niño
subtitle: Experts warn that extreme weather could keep grocery costs high for years to come

summary.en: A powerful weather event known as a 'super' El Niño is expected to significantly impact global food supplies. Economists warn that this could lead to a major price shock that lasts until 2028. The event follows existing economic pressures caused by the war in Iran. Experts note that the effects will be uneven, with some regions facing drought while others may see different results. Central banks are now watching these developments closely as they manage interest rates.
summary.zh: 一種被稱為「超級聖嬰現象」的強大天氣事件，預計將對全球糧食供應產生重大影響。經濟學家警告，這可能導致持續至二〇二八年的重大價格衝擊。此事件發生在伊朗戰爭所引發的既有經濟壓力之下。專家指出，其影響將不均衡，部分地區面臨乾旱，而其他地區則可能出現不同結果。各國央行在管理利率的同時，正密切關注這些發展。

paragraphs.en: A powerful weather pattern known as a 'super' El Niño is set to hit the world this year, and experts are warning that it could cause a major shock to global food prices. This event, which involves warmer water spreading across the Pacific Ocean, is expected to be one of the strongest on record. Economists suggest that the consequences of this weather cycle could last until 2028, creating a long-term challenge for households already struggling with high living costs.
paragraphs.zh: 一種被稱為「超級聖嬰現象」的強大天氣型態預計將於今年席捲全球，專家警告這可能對全球糧食價格造成重大衝擊。此現象涉及太平洋海域水溫升高，預計將成為有紀錄以來最強勁的一次。經濟學家指出，此天氣週期的影響可能持續至二〇二八年，對於已在為高昂生活成本所苦的家庭而言，將構成一項長期挑戰。

paragraphs.en: This update follows earlier reports from July 2026, which first highlighted the risks posed by this climate event. According to The Guardian, the world is currently facing two major problems at once: the ongoing war in Iran and the threat of extreme weather linked to global heating. The war has already pushed food prices to their highest level in three years. Now, the arrival of a 'super' El Niño threatens to make these supply chain problems even worse.
paragraphs.zh: 此項更新延續了二〇二六年七月的早期報導，當時即首度強調了此氣候事件帶來的風險。根據《衛報》（The Guardian）報導，全球目前正面臨兩大難題：持續進行中的伊朗戰爭，以及與全球暖化相關的極端天氣威脅。戰爭已將糧食價格推升至三年來的新高，而如今「超級聖嬰現象」的到來，恐將使這些供應鏈問題雪上加霜。

paragraphs.en: The US National Oceanic and Atmospheric Administration (NOAA) has confirmed that warming conditions are taking hold in the Pacific. They estimate a 63% chance that sea surface temperatures will rise more than 2C above normal levels later this year. This change in temperature is expected to cause heatwaves, flooding, and storms, all of which can damage crops and disrupt the global food supply network.
paragraphs.zh: 美國國家海洋暨大氣總署（NOAA）已證實，太平洋地區正出現暖化跡象。該機構預估，今年稍晚海面溫度高於正常水準攝氏兩度以上的機率為百分之六十三。此溫度變化預計將引發熱浪、洪水與風暴，這些災害均可能損害農作物並擾亂全球糧食供應網絡。

paragraphs.en: Analysts at Goldman Sachs have predicted that the strength of this El Niño could cause a 15.8% surge in global food commodity prices. These are the raw materials like wheat, corn, and rice that are used to make the food we buy. This rise would have a 'knock-on effect'—a situation where one event causes a series of other events—across the world. For example, consumers in the eurozone could see their food prices rise by 1.3% as a result of these global shifts.
paragraphs.zh: 高盛集團（Goldman Sachs）的分析師預測，此次聖嬰現象的強度可能導致全球糧食大宗商品價格飆升百分之十五點八。這些商品包括小麥、玉米與稻米等用於製造日常食品的原料。此漲幅將產生「連鎖效應」，即由單一事件引發一連串後續反應，影響遍及全球。例如，歐元區的消費者可能因這些全球性的變動，面臨糧食價格上漲百分之一點三的情況。

paragraphs.en: However, the full impact of this weather event will not be felt immediately. Experts at Goldman Sachs explained that the consequences could take until the second half of 2028 to be fully realized. This delay happens because different crops have different planting, growing, and harvesting cycles. Additionally, logistical challenges, such as changes in water levels in canals and rivers used to ship food, will take time to affect the final price on store shelves.
paragraphs.zh: 然而，此天氣事件的全面影響不會立即顯現。高盛的專家解釋，其後果可能要到二〇二八年下半年才會完全浮現。這種延遲是因為不同農作物有各自的種植、生長與收成週期。此外，物流方面的挑戰，例如用於運送糧食的運河與河流水位變化，也需要時間才會反映在商店貨架的最終售價上。

paragraphs.en: Not every part of the world will be affected in the same way. Analysts at the Swiss bank UBS noted that El Niño does not affect agriculture uniformly. Instead, it reshapes rainfall and temperature patterns, creating different outcomes for different areas. While some regions may face devastating droughts, others might experience weather conditions that are actually helpful for their specific crops. Historically, severe El Niño events have caused catastrophic droughts in places like Brazil, India, and southern Africa, leading to serious food shortages.
paragraphs.zh: 並非世界各地都會受到同樣的影響。瑞士銀行（UBS）的分析師指出，聖嬰現象對農業的影響並非一致。相反地，它會重塑降雨與溫度型態，對不同地區產生迥異的結果。雖然部分地區可能面臨毀滅性的乾旱，但其他地區可能會經歷對特定作物有利的天氣條件。歷史上，嚴重的聖嬰現象曾在巴西、印度與非洲南部等地造成災難性乾旱，導致嚴重的糧食短缺。

paragraphs.en: This situation is also causing concern for central banks, which are the institutions that manage a country's money and interest rates. Because the rising cost of food is a major part of inflation—the general increase in prices over time—the prospect of a new price shock is making it difficult for these banks to lower interest rates. Analysts at the Italian bank UniCredit wrote that El Niño puts 'climateflation' back on the agenda. This term refers to the way climate change and extreme weather can directly cause prices to go up.
paragraphs.zh: 此局勢也引起了負責管理國家貨幣與利率的中央銀行之擔憂。由於糧食成本上漲是通貨膨脹（即物價隨時間普遍上漲的現象）的重要組成部分，新一波價格衝擊的前景，使得這些銀行難以調降利率。義大利裕信銀行（UniCredit）的分析師撰文指出，聖嬰現象讓「氣候通膨」重新成為焦點。此術語指的是氣候變遷與極端天氣直接導致物價上漲的現象。

paragraphs.en: As the world prepares for this 'super' El Niño, the combination of war-related supply issues and climate-driven weather events creates a complex environment. With fertilizer and energy supplies already limited, even small disruptions to the food supply could lead to significant problems. For now, governments and businesses are watching the Pacific closely, hoping to understand how these shifting weather patterns will change the cost of living for people around the globe in the coming years.
paragraphs.zh: 隨著全球準備迎接此「超級聖嬰現象」，戰爭相關的供應問題與氣候驅動的天氣事件相結合，創造了一個複雜的環境。在肥料與能源供應本已受限的情況下，即便糧食供應出現微小擾動，也可能導致重大問題。目前，各國政府與企業正密切關注太平洋的動態，希望能了解這些變動的天氣型態，將如何在未來幾年改變全球民眾的生活成本。

keyTerms.definitionEn: A sudden, unexpected, and unpleasant event or change.
keyTerms.definitionZh: 衝擊、震撼（指突發且負面的事件）。
keyTerms.noteZh: 常見作動詞（震驚），這裡作名詞，指經濟上的劇烈波動。文中：experts are warning that it could cause a major shock to global food prices.

keyTerms.definitionEn: To become established or to start to have an effect.
keyTerms.definitionZh: 開始產生影響、站穩腳跟。
keyTerms.noteZh: 由動詞 take 和名詞 hold 組成，這裡指氣候現象開始發威。文中：The US National Oceanic and Atmospheric Administration (NOAA) has confirmed that warming conditions are taking hold in the Pacific.

keyTerms.definitionEn: A situation where an event or process causes a series of other events to happen.
keyTerms.noteZh: 指一件事引發後續一連串的影響，非字面上的敲擊。文中：This rise would have a 'knock-on effect'—a situation where one event causes a series of other events—across the world.

keyTerms.definitionEn: A list of matters to be discussed or dealt with.
keyTerms.noteZh: 常見於會議議程，這裡指氣候通膨重新成為各界關注與討論的重點。文中：Analysts at the Italian bank UniCredit wrote that El Niño puts 'climateflation' back on the agenda.

sources.name: The Guardian Environment
sources.title: ‘Super’ El Niño could cause global food price shock lasting into 2028, analysts say

previousArticles.title: A 'Super' El Niño Could Make Food Prices Rise Until 2028
previousArticles.summaryEn: A powerful weather event known as a 'super' El Niño is expected to hit the world this year. Experts warn that this could cause a major shock to global food prices. The impact of this weather pattern may last until 2028. Many countries are already dealing with high living costs due to other global conflicts. Central banks are concerned that this will keep interest rates high for a longer time.

quiz.question: According to the NOAA, what is the specific condition expected to occur in the Pacific later this year?

options.A: A 15.8% increase in global food commodity prices.
options.B: A 63% probability that sea surface temperatures will exceed normal levels by more than 2C.
options.C: A complete disruption of the global food supply network due to war.
options.D: A permanent shift in rainfall patterns that will affect all agricultural regions equally.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 B。根據文章第三段，美國國家海洋暨大氣總署（NOAA）明確指出，海平面溫度上升超過正常值 2C 的機率估計為 63%。選項 A 是高盛銀行的預測，而非 NOAA 的數據；選項 C 雖提到供應鏈中斷，但未提及戰爭是唯一原因；選項 D 則與第六段提到的「不均勻影響」矛盾。

quiz.question: Why might the full economic impact of the 'super' El Niño be delayed until 2028?

options.A: Because central banks are actively lowering interest rates to prevent inflation.
options.B: Because the war in Iran is expected to end before the weather patterns fully develop.
options.C: Because the biological cycles of crops and logistical challenges in shipping take time to manifest.
options.D: Because global food commodity prices are currently at their lowest level in three years.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 C。根據第五段，氣候事件的影響不會立即顯現，因為農作物有不同的生長與收成週期，加上運輸物流（如運河水位變化）的影響需要時間累積，才會反映在最終零售價格上。選項 A 與文意相反，中央銀行正因通膨壓力而難以降息；選項 B 文章未提及戰爭結束時間；選項 D 與第二段提到的價格處於三年高點矛盾。

quiz.question: In the fourth paragraph, what does the term 'knock-on effect' mean?

options.A: A sudden stop in production caused by a lack of raw materials.
options.B: A chain reaction where one event triggers a series of subsequent consequences.
options.C: A significant reduction in the cost of living for consumers in the eurozone.
options.D: A direct correlation between war and the decrease in global food prices.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 B。根據第四段上下文，作者將 'knock-on effect' 定義為「一個事件導致一系列其他事件的情況」，即連鎖反應。選項 A 指的是供應中斷，而非連鎖效應；選項 C 錯誤地將其描述為生活成本降低；選項 D 則錯誤地將其與戰爭導致價格下降連結，這與文中提到的價格上漲事實不符。

quiz.question: What is the primary message of the article regarding the upcoming 'super' El Niño?

options.A: It is a weather event that will primarily benefit the agricultural sectors of Brazil and India.
options.B: It is a temporary climate issue that will have no long-term impact on global interest rates.
options.C: It acts as a compounding threat to global food security and economic stability alongside existing geopolitical issues.
options.D: It is the sole cause of the current inflation crisis being managed by central banks worldwide.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 C。本文主旨旨在探討 'super' El Niño 如何與現有的地緣政治（如戰爭）及供應鏈問題疊加，對全球糧食價格與經濟穩定造成長期挑戰。選項 A 忽略了該事件帶來的負面衝擊；選項 B 忽略了其對通膨與利率的長期影響；選項 D 錯誤地將其視為通膨的唯一成因，忽略了文章提到的戰爭等其他因素。
